data.narrative:
Addionics is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Moshiel Biton and Farid Tariq, having raised $39M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company operating in the same sector and stage, including peers such as Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised ($39M) as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B to subsequent-stage progression, yields an implied valuation of $74M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $355M, with the peer set ranging from $120M (floor) to $5.0B (Commonwealth Fusion Systems). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous technology maturity and market expectations within the climate tech sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $63M to $408M, with a median point estimate of $215M. The 6.5x spread between low and high bounds reflects material uncertainty around Addionics' competitive positioning relative to the peer set, technology readiness level, and market capture assumptions. Stage-progression suggests a conservative floor, while peer-median anchoring indicates substantially higher market comparables. Resolution of this range depends on visibility into Addionics' unit economics, pathway to commercialization, and differentiation versus named peers.
